purpose: Public machine-readable contract for the Record-Chain Intake Gateway. This is the only public submission method for external agents.

health.description: Liveness probe. Returns 200 when the gateway process is running.

readiness.description: Readiness probe. Returns 200 when the gateway can accept submissions, 503 otherwise.

preflight.description: Validates a submission without persisting it. Returns diagnostics, warnings, and route detection. Use before submit to catch errors cheaply.

submit.description: Persists a submission as an intake record and returns a receipt. This is the only way to submit records to the Record-Chain.

receipt.description: Retrieves the receipt for a previously submitted intake record by its receipt_id.

receipt_id.description: The receipt identifier returned by the submit endpoint.

public_submission_rule.description: External agents submit exclusively through this gateway's HTTP API. All server-side pipeline steps (validation, hashing, chain append, receipt generation) are handled internally. Agents must never attempt to bypass the gateway.

server_side_pipeline.description: The gateway owns the full pipeline: preflight validates, submit persists to intake, internal workers append to the canonical Record-Chain, and hashes are assigned server-side. A receipt confirms intake acceptance but does not guarantee final chain position.

boundary.description: The gateway is a submission interface only. It holds no authority, performs no governance, issues no attestations, does not receive successor declarations, does not process amendments, and defers to Bitcoin-original records in all conflicts.
